heading: Executive Department; Secretary
label: 2202 Executive Department; Secretary
text: The Department of Agriculture shall be an executive department, under the supervision and control of a Secretary of Agriculture, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. The provisions of title 4 of the Revised Statutes, including all amendments thereto, shall be applicable to said department; and all laws and parts of laws relating to the Department of Agriculture in existence , as far as the same are applicable and not in conflict with this section, and only so far, are continued in full force and effect.February 9, 1889
notes: References in TextTitle 4 of the Revised Statutes, referred to in text, was entitled “Provisions Applicable to All Executive Departments, and consisted of R.S. §§ 158 to 198. For provisions of the Code derived from such title 4, see sections 101, 301, 303, 304, 503, 2952, 3101, 3106, 3341, 3345 to 3349, 5535, 5536 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees; , Crimes and Criminal Procedure; sections 514, 520 of Title 28, Judiciary and Judicial Procedure; , Money and Finance.section 207 of Title 18section 3321 of Title 31
